rules.intervention_detail: Enrol the student in a structured academic support or tutoring programme. Assign a faculty mentor to monitor progress weekly. Consider a reduced course load for the next semester.
Flag student for early academic review. Connect with subject teachers to identify specific areas of difficulty. Arrange supplementary classes or peer tutoring.
Schedule a meeting with the student to review their subject choices and workload. Assess whether the course difficulty matches the student's preparation level. Explore bridging courses if needed.
Conduct an academic review meeting after the first semester. Identify subject-specific struggles and assign peer mentors. Set academic improvement goals for semester 2.
Work with the student to create a personal study plan. Provide access to past exam papers, revision materials, and study skills workshops. Monitor grades monthly.
Assess the student for foundational knowledge gaps. Provide access to remedial learning resources. Consider recommending a course audit before re-enrollment.
The student's grades have dropped between semesters. Conduct a wellbeing and academic check-in. Identify any personal, financial, or health-related factors contributing to the decline.
Place the student on an academic improvement plan. Schedule bi-weekly check-ins with their advisor. Provide access to learning support services and consider a reduced unit load.

Connect the student with the financial aid office immediately. Explore available scholarships, bursaries, emergency funds, or payment plan options. Ensure overdue fees do not result in sudden deregistration.
Refer the student to a financial counsellor to manage existing debt. Investigate eligibility for institutional debt relief programmes or government financial assistance.

The student faces multiple financial stressors. Coordinate between financial aid, academic advising, and student services to create a holistic support plan. Explore part-time work-study opportunities on campus.
Inform the student about available scholarship and bursary opportunities. Assist with scholarship applications and connect them with the financial aid advisor.
Connect the student with on-campus housing services or affordable accommodation near the institution. Provide information about transport subsidies and commuting options.

The student has significantly reduced their enrolled units. Reach out proactively to understand the reason. Offer flexible study options, online learning alternatives, or a leave of absence if needed.
Low evaluation count indicates disengagement from coursework. Contact the student to understand barriers to participation. Connect with student wellbeing services if personal issues are identified.
The student's approval rate is worsening across semesters. Arrange a meeting to discuss barriers. Explore counselling, study skills training, or course load adjustment.

Mature students often face competing responsibilities such as employment or family obligations. Connect with the mature student support programme. Explore flexible scheduling, online learning options, or part-time study.

Connect the student with the international student office. Provide language support, cultural orientation resources, and peer buddy programmes. Ensure visa and fee obligations are clearly communicated.

Ensure the student has access to all entitled disability support services. Review whether current accommodations (extended time, assistive technology, alternative formats) are adequate and being utilised.

High regional unemployment may reduce the perceived value of continuing education. Connect the student with career services to explore graduate employment prospects and the long-term benefits of completing their qualification.
